result:
I’d make it so every person could reliably understand and care about one another’s inner experience.

A lot of the world’s worst problems—violence, cruelty, exploitation, neglect, even some forms of inequality—grow when people stop seeing others as fully real. If people could genuinely feel the weight of another person’s fear, hope, pain, and dignity, I think we’d make kinder laws, build fairer systems, and treat each other with more patience.

Not perfect agreement—just deeper empathy, paired with the ability to act on it.

What about you?

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
